summary: IDF engaged Hezbollah militants on the Ali al‑Taher ridge in southern Lebanon, reporting a neutralized threat. Hezbollah publicly declared Israel's project in Lebanon broken and called for full Israeli withdrawal. US political statements referenced Iran's nuclear stance and regional oil flows.
key_points: IDF forces fired on Hezbollah militants crossing the security zone on the Ali al‑Taher ridge, identifying four armed attackers on a bulldozer and motorcycle and reporting a hit (Source: @idf_telegram/24424).
Hezbollah Secretary‑General Sheikh Naim Qassem praised Iran and declared the Israeli project in Lebanon broken, urging full Israeli withdrawal (Source: @presstv/195324).
US Senate passed a resolution directing President Trump to withdraw US forces from the war with Iran (Source: @presstv/195329).
Former US President Trump stated Iran will not be allowed to acquire nuclear weapons and mentioned large oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @VahidOnline/76631).
Hezbollah claimed Israeli fire hit civilians clearing roads in southern Lebanon, calling it a ceasefire violation (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4231).
A Palestinian official condemned vandalisation of the Ibrahimi Mosque courtyard in Hebron, West Bank (Source: @presstv/195333).
An Israeli bus caught fire on Highway 77 near the Zrazir interchange, prompting firefighting response (Source: @N12chat/214315).
A robot bomb was detonated by Israeli forces east of Gaza City (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372607).

sections.executive_summary: IDF engaged Hezbollah militants on the Ali al‑Taher ridge, reporting a neutralized threat. Hezbollah declared Israel's project in Lebanon broken and called for full withdrawal. US political moves addressed Iran's nuclear ambitions and regional oil flows.

points: Former US President Trump asserted Iran will not obtain nuclear weapons and highlighted a record oil flow of 19 million barrels through the Strait of Hormuz, describing it as the largest in history (Source: @VahidOnline/76631).
Trump reiterated that the US will prevent Iran from acquiring nuclear capability, stating the US will act if Iran seeks nuclear weapons (Source: @naya_foriraq/79749).
US Senate adopted a resolution directing President Trump to withdraw US forces from the war with Iran (Source: @presstv/195329).
Hezbollah Secretary‑General praised Iran’s leadership and armed forces, indicating continued Iranian influence in the region (Source: @presstv/195336).

IDF reported firing on Hezbollah militants crossing the security zone on the Ali al‑Taher ridge, identifying four armed attackers on a bulldozer and motorcycle and confirming a hit (Source: @idf_telegram/24424).
IDF announced another engagement north of the security zone, targeting armed militants near its forces on the Ali al‑Taher ridge and stating the threat was removed (Source: @idf_telegram/24423).
Hezbollah claimed Israeli fire hit civilians clearing roads in southern Lebanon, labeling it a ceasefire violation (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4231).
An Israeli bus caught fire on Highway 77 near the Zrazir interchange, prompting firefighting operations (Source: @N12chat/214315).
A Palestinian ministry condemned Israeli actions vandalising the courtyard of the Ibrahimi Mosque in Hebron, West Bank (Source: @presstv/195333).
Israeli forces detonated a robot bomb east of Gaza City (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372606).
Hezbollah Secretary‑General Sheikh Naim Qassem declared the Israeli project in Lebanon broken and urged full Israeli withdrawal (Source: @presstv/195324).
Hezbollah praised Iran and its leadership, reinforcing the alliance between the groups (Source: @presstv/195336).
Hezbollah accused Israeli fire of hitting civilians clearing roads in southern Lebanon (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4231).
Reports of a vehicle attack in the Bint Jbeil area were noted (Source: @yediotnews25/131838).
No explicit operational details regarding Syria or Iraq were present in the source messages.
Trump referenced large oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz and discussed US‑Iran negotiations, indicating Gulf maritime activity (Source: @VahidOnline/76631).

key_developments: IDF neutralised four Hezbollah militants on the Ali al‑Taher ridge, reporting a hit on the attackers (Source: @idf_telegram/24424).
Hezbollah declared Israel's project in Lebanon broken and called for full Israeli withdrawal (Source: @presstv/195324).
US Senate resolution directed President Trump to withdraw US forces from the war with Iran (Source: @presstv/195329).
Former President Trump asserted Iran will not acquire nuclear weapons and highlighted record oil flow through the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @VahidOnline/76631).
Hezbollah praised Iran’s leadership, indicating continued Iranian influence in Lebanon (Source: @presstv/195336).
Palestinian ministry condemned Israeli vandalism at the Ibrahimi Mosque courtyard in Hebron (Source: @presstv/195333).
Israeli forces detonated a robot bomb east of Gaza City (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372606).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah remains active on the Lebanon‑Israel border, employing militants in civilian cover and engaging Israeli forces on the Ali al‑Taher ridge. US political actions signal potential shifts in the Iran‑related conflict, though no direct military engagements were reported. Israeli operations continue to target perceived immediate threats, maintaining a high operational tempo in southern Lebanon.
sections.regional_response: Lebanese factions, represented by Hezbollah, are publicly challenging Israeli presence and calling for withdrawal, while praising Iranian support. US legislative moves aim to limit involvement in Iran, reflecting domestic pressure to de‑escalate. Palestinian authorities are reacting to Israeli actions in the West Bank, indicating heightened tensions on multiple fronts.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah movements near the Ali al‑Taher ridge is critical, as militants use civilian infrastructure for concealment. US statements on Iran's nuclear program and oil flows may affect regional maritime security in the Strait of Hormuz. Continued Israeli‑Hezbollah engagements could trigger broader escalation if civilian casualties increase.
